zh.lifeSummary: 當Louis de SULLY於大約1330年出生在法國崔-瓦爾 德 洛易洛易特素利-素-洛易時，他的父親Jean I de SULLY30歲，母親Marguerite de Bourbon24歲。他於1354年3月10日與Isabeau de Craon在法國崔-瓦爾 德 洛易洛易特素利-素-洛易結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1382年11月29日在他的家鄉享年53歲，埋葬在法國崔傑梅-斯-波斯。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: SULLY
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armelle, Christophe, Geralde, Luce, Pierre, Reynald.
English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from any of the three places called Sully in Calvados (Normandy), Aisne (Picardy), and Loiret (Centre); see 6 below.

English: of Norman origin, perhaps a habitational name from Sully (Glamorgan). However, the placename is manorial in origin, from the Norman surname in 1 above, which is recorded in Glamorgan in the 12th century.

variants.name: Soley
meanings: Catalan: variant of Solell, a topographic name from solell ‘sunny side, southern slope’, from a derivative of sol ‘sun’. Compare Sol 2.

nameMeanings.name: Louis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hlōd ‘fame’ + wīg ‘war’. It was very common in French royal and noble families. Louis I ( 778–840 ) was the son of Charlemagne, who ruled as both King of France and Holy Roman Emperor. Altogether, the name was borne by sixteen kings of France up to the French Revolution, in which Louis XVI perished. Louis XIV, ‘the Sun King’ ( 1638–1715 ), reigned for seventy-two years ( 1643–1715 ), presiding in the middle part of his reign over a period of unparalleled French power and prosperity. In modern times Louis is also found in the English-speaking world (usually pronounced ‘loo-ee’). In Britain the Anglicized form Lewis is rather more common, whereas in America the reverse is true.

variants.name: Lewie
meanings: Anglicized spelling of   Louis  , or a pet form of   Lewis .

variants.name: Louie
meanings: Variant spelling of   Lewie   or   Louis  , currently in fashion.

variants.name: Lewi
meanings: name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hlōd ‘fame’ + wīg ‘war’. It was very common in French royal and noble families. Louis I ( 778–840 ) was the son of Charlemagne, who ruled as both King of France and Holy Roman Emperor. Altogether, the name was borne by sixteen kings of France up to the French Revolution, in which Louis XVI perished. Louis XIV, ‘the Sun King’ ( 1638–1715 ), reigned for seventy-two years ( 1643–1715 ), presiding in the middle part of his reign over a period of unparalleled French power and prosperity. In modern times Louis is also found in the English-speaking world (usually pronounced ‘loo-ee’). In Britain the Anglicized form Lewis is rather more common, whereas in America the reverse is true.

en.lifeSummary: When Louis de SULLY was born about 1330, in Sully-sur-Loire, Loiret, Centre-Val de Loire, France, his father, Jean I de SULLY, was 30 and his mother, Marguerite de Bourbon, was 24. He married Isabeau de Craon on 10 March 1354, in Sully-sur-Loire, Loiret, Centre-Val de Loire, France. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died on 29 November 1382, in his hometown, at the age of 53, and was buried in Méry-ès-Bois, Cher, Centre, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Louis de SULLY est né environ 1330, à Sully-sur-Loire, Loiret, Centre-Val de Loire, France, son père, Jean I de SULLY, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Marguerite de Bourbon, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Isabeau de Craon le 10 mars 1354, à Sully-sur-Loire, Loiret, Centre-Val de Loire, France. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé le 29 novembre 1382, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 53 ans, et a été enterré à Méry-ès-Bois, Cher, Centre, France.

ja.lifeSummary: Louis de SULLYが約1330年にフランスセントレ-ヴァル ド ロイアーロイアーエトスリ-スル-ロイアーで生まれた時，父Jean I de SULLYは30歳，母Marguerite de Bourbonは24歳であった。1354年3月10日にフランスセントレ-ヴァル ド ロイアーロイアーエトスリ-スル-ロイアーでIsabeau de Craonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1382年11月29日に故郷で53歳で死去し，フランスセントアチャームeリ-eス-ボイスにて埋葬された。

ko.Sully-sur-Loire,45315,Loiret,Centre-Val: 프랑스 세트레-발 데 로이르 로이렛 설리-수-로이르

ko.lifeSummary: Louis de SULLY(이)가 대략 1330년 프랑스 세트레-발 데 로이르 로이렛 설리-수-로이르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Jean I de SULLY(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Marguerite de Bourbon(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1354년 3월 10일 프랑스 세트레-발 데 로이르 로이렛 설리-수-로이르에서 Isabeau de Craon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1382년 11월 29일 그의 고향에서 53세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 세트르 처 e리-e스-보이스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Louis de SULLY nasceu aproximadamente 1330, em Sully-sur-Loire, Loiret, Centre-Val de Loire, França, seu pai, Jean I de SULLY, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Marguerite de Bourbon, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Isabeau de Craon em 10 de março de 1354, em Sully-sur-Loire, Loiret, Centre-Val de Loire, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 29 de novembro de 1382, em sua cidade natal, com 53 anos, e foi sepultado em Méry-ès-Bois, Cher, Centre, França.

сулли-сур-лоир, лоирет, кентре-валь де лоир, Франция
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Louis de SULLY родился около 1330 г. в местности сулли-сур-лоир, лоирет, кентре-валь де лоир, Франция, его отцу по имени Jean I de SULLY было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Marguerite de Bourbon, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabeau de Craon 10 марта 1354 г. в местности сулли-сур-лоир, лоирет, кентре-валь де лоир, Франция. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер 29 ноября 1382 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 53 лет и был похоронен в местности мeри-eс-боис, чир, кентр, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Louis de SULLY nació aproximadamente 1330, en Sully-sur-Loire, Loiret, Centre-Val de Loire, Francia, su papá, Jean I de SULLY, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Marguerite de Bourbon, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Isabeau de Craon el 10 de marzo de 1354, en Sully-sur-Loire, Loiret, Centre-Val de Loire, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió el 29 de noviembre de 1382, en su pueblo, a la edad de 53 años, y fue sepultado en Méry-ès-Bois, Cher, Centre, Francia.
